evals.name: command-surface-restores-existing-commands
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit v7.2 active command surface. Describe the user-facing commands. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should list /tk:spec, /tk:gap, /tk:reflect, /tk:handoff, and /tk:meta-feedback as active user-facing commands. Should explain that handoff and meta-feedback were preserved existing commands, not newly invented v7 commands. Should explain that spec/gap write branch-local working memory, reflect creates durable insight, handoff creates continuation context, and meta-feedback extracts generalized TigerKit improvement feedback from session history.
expectations: Lists /tk:spec
Lists /tk:gap
Lists /tk:reflect
Lists /tk:handoff
Lists /tk:meta-feedback
Treats handoff as restored existing continuation command
Treats meta-feedback as restored existing session-history improvement command
Explains spec/gap branch-local working memory
Explains reflect-only durable insight

evals.name: gap-default-recommends-lite-or-strict
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit v7.2 /tk:gap when a user calls `/tk:gap` with mixed Jira, current implementation, and design notes for a small ticket. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should skim user-provided references, target hints, current implementation candidates, and risk signals first. Should compute risk score and side-effect confidence, then run the selected preset automatically. Default posture should be strict. Lite should be recommended only when side-effect confidence is at least 90, risk score is at most 15, and no hard strict trigger exists. Lite is the fast v7 contract-based preset with bounded target resolving and bounded usage lookup, not a legacy alias. Strict is the expanded preset with broader discovery and one red-team pass for 신규 화면/flow, validation, permission, data mutation, auth, payment, destructive action, shared component, inaccessible source, source conflict, release gate, or high ambiguity risk. Changed files are current implementation evidence candidates, not the primary scope. Source or plan classes that are absent should cause related agent dispatch skips with reasons recorded. Candidate file:line or module-path evidence should be read back against the current target surface before JudgeMergerAgent; stale coordinates should be repaired or downgraded to low_confidence/missing_evidence/unverifiable instead of silently passed. Stdout should show executed preset and reason by default, show recommended preset only when it differs from executed preset, and include compact tables for Actionable Findings plus Rejected/Downgraded observations so the session shows what valid gaps mean without printing the full report. The result should include a rerun trail for a different preset when useful, and should avoid confusing labels like `선택모드: lite 실행모드: lite`.
expectations: Skims user-provided references and target hints before selecting preset
Treats changed files as current implementation evidence candidates, not primary scope
Computes risk score and side-effect confidence
Runs the selected preset automatically when no preset flag is provided
Defaults toward strict unless lite confidence is very high
Recommends lite only when side-effect confidence >= 90 and risk score <= 15
Explains lite is bounded contract-based review
Explains strict is expanded discovery plus one red-team pass
Treats 신규 화면 or 신규 flow as strict trigger
Records dispatch skips when source or plan classes are absent
Runs an evidence precision gate before JudgeMergerAgent
Repairs or downgrades stale file:line or module-path evidence instead of silently passing it
Leaves rerun trail for useful alternate preset
Shows executed preset and reason by default
Shows recommended preset only when it differs from executed preset
Shows compact Actionable Findings table in stdout
Shows compact Rejected/Downgraded table in stdout
Does not use confusing selection/execution wording
Does not mention --legacy as an active mode
Explains legacy is v6-era unsupported behavior, not a lite alias
Does not mention --deep as an active mode
Does not mention --no-strict as an active mode

evals.name: gap-strict-for-risky-backend-work
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit v7.2 /tk:gap on a backend Jira ticket touching validation and data mutation. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should recommend and run strict when no preset flag is provided because backend validation and data mutation increase false-positive and missed-edge-case risk. If the user explicitly passed --lite, it should still run lite and leave a strict rerun trail. It should require concrete source contract and target implementation evidence before accepting backend findings. It should avoid inferring DTO, permission, persistence, or state-transition semantics without source support. It should not infer producer absence from consumer-side UI shape, fallback/default branches, empty states, mocks, fixtures, mappers, or missing consumer usage alone. Producer-absence candidates should require direct producer-side evidence such as API contract, schema, serializer, endpoint response, data model, persistence logic, backend test fixture, or owner-confirmed behavior before they can become P-level findings or SourceConflict. If producer-side evidence is missing, it should reject or downgrade with missing_producer_evidence, missing_evidence, or unverifiable. If the API contract is ambiguous, even when one interpretation looks likely, it should create Clarification Needed, SourceConflict, or rejected unverifiable/missing_evidence instead of accepting a final finding until user consent or owner confirmation exists. Strict should also apply source/plan dispatch skips when classes are absent.
expectations: Recommends strict for backend validation or data mutation
Runs strict automatically when strict is the recommended preset and no preset flag is provided
Honors explicit --lite while leaving strict rerun trail
Requires source contract evidence for backend findings
Requires target implementation evidence for backend findings
Does not infer DTO semantics from compatible names alone
Does not infer permission, persistence, or state transition behavior without evidence
Does not infer producer absence from consumer-side fallback/default/empty paths alone
Requires direct producer-side evidence before accepting producer-absence findings or SourceConflict
Rejects or downgrades producer-absence claims without producer evidence as missing_producer_evidence, missing_evidence, or unverifiable
Routes unclear backend semantics to Clarification Needed, SourceConflict, rejected unverifiable, or rejected missing_evidence instead of accepted finding
Requires user consent or owner confirmation before treating ambiguous API meaning as confirmed
Records dispatch skips when source or plan classes are absent

evals.name: meta-feedback-uses-session-history-for-gap-improvements
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit v7.2 /tk:meta-feedback after a session where the user said gap took 15 minutes on a small ticket, backend findings had false positives, mode wording was confusing, and ambiguous UI/API decisions should be resolved with the user. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should use the session history as evidence, generalize the feedback without exposing project assets, and propose TigerKit command or skill improvements. It should capture performance friction for gap preset selection, strict-default scoring, dispatch gate needs, candidate evidence precision gates for stale file:line/module-path coordinates, producer-absence evidence gates for backend false-positive reduction, rerun trail UX, ambiguity consent gate guidance, and UI clarification via tables or aligned TUI/ASCII mockups. It should not create repo rule patches like reflect and should not output raw user quotes, paths, tickets, PR numbers, branch names, or project-specific labels.
expectations: Uses session history as the evidence source
Generalizes gap performance friction
Generalizes strict-default scoring improvement
Generalizes dispatch gate improvement
Generalizes candidate evidence precision gate improvement
Generalizes producer-absence evidence gate improvement
Generalizes rerun trail and mode wording UX improvement
Generalizes backend false-positive feedback
Generalizes ambiguity consent gate feedback
Mentions tables or aligned TUI/ASCII mockups for UI clarification
Proposes TigerKit command or skill improvement
Does not expose raw user quotes
Does not expose paths, tickets, PR numbers, branch names, or project-specific labels
Does not behave like /tk:reflect repo rule patching

evals.name: handoff-remains-continuation-command
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit v7.2 /tk:handoff after a branch has spec and gap artifacts. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should preserve /tk:handoff as the existing continuation command. It should write .claude/handoffs/current.md by default, include Reader Guide and Resume Prompt, optionally mention latest branch-local Spec Patch and Gap Run paths when observed, and create a dated archive only when archive=true or an explicit archive request is present.
expectations: Preserves /tk:handoff as active command
Writes .claude/handoffs/current.md by default
Includes Reader Guide
Includes Resume Prompt
Can reference latest branch-local Spec Patch and Gap Run paths
Does not create dated archive without archive=true or explicit archive request

evals.name: reflect-applies-directly-to-rules-and-claude
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit v7.2 /tk:reflect after branch-local gap runs produced durable repo-wide insight candidates. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should default to apply=true, read branch-local TigerKit memory, write directly to CLAUDE.md or .claude/rules/* depending on target scope, treat --dry-run and --apply=false as preview-only aliases, avoid modifying source code, dedupe repeated insights, and avoid creating a separate tigerkit-reflections sidecar file.
expectations: Defaults /tk:reflect to apply=true
Treats --dry-run as apply=false
Treats --apply=false as dry-run alias
Reads branch-local specs/gap memory
Writes directly to CLAUDE.md or .claude/rules/*
Does not modify source code
Does not create tigerkit-reflections sidecar file
Skips duplicate insights
Prints apply summary

evals.name: gap-ambiguity-requires-user-consent
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit v7.2 /tk:gap on a ticket where Product Spec and API contract can be interpreted two ways, and a UI decision has two plausible layouts. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should not choose a likely Product/API/Design interpretation as confirmed by itself. It should explore according to the selected preset, then mark ambiguous decision paths as Clarification Needed, SourceConflict, or rejected unverifiable/missing_evidence until user consent or owner confirmation exists. It should continue clear checks. Questions should be split into implementation-blocking and reference-only. For UI ambiguity it should use a compact option/evidence/impact/recommendation table and, when helpful, an aligned TUI/ASCII prototype whose right borders do not drift with Korean text.
expectations: Does not self-confirm ambiguous Product Spec interpretation
Does not self-confirm ambiguous API contract interpretation
Does not accept final finding from ambiguous interpretation without user consent or owner confirmation
Uses Clarification Needed, SourceConflict, rejected unverifiable, or rejected missing_evidence for ambiguous paths
Continues clear non-ambiguous checks
Splits questions into implementation-blocking and reference-only
Includes options, evidence, impact, recommendation, and status in clarification questions
Uses aligned TUI/ASCII prototype for UI ambiguity when helpful
Keeps right borders aligned for Korean text
